reviews.review_description:
It hard to find any fault to this reader, it does almost everything I could dream of a comic and book reader in VR. First, on the VR side, it includes several beautiful 360 environments, many of them complete with sound effects like waves and relaxing music, so it makes reading a fantastic experience.

 In terms of reading modes, it has one or two page read, great if you want to zoom in a comic or a book, and for me this is fantastic. Since Oculus GO resolution is low, zooming in allows to enjoy the beatiful art without constantly moving your head from one side to another. It also has night mode that helps reading books be less annoying for the eyes. The controller is well used and it's easy to move around within the book/comic and with the interface.

 The library has big icons and it's really nice, you can switch between comics and books so it's less cluttered. Once in the library, they load pretty fast.

 Importing is a bit of a mixed bag. Importing comics takes a second, but epub books take a while, the good thing is that you can keep reading a book or a comic while it's importing, so it's not much of an issue. It's also well documented, with an extensive instruction manual explaining everything you need to know.

 I haven't tested audiobooks or music importing yet, but it's fantastic that it also includes these features if you have a music collection that you like to listen or are a fan of audiobooks. 

 The only issue I see now is that with the latest version I can't browse my Oculus GO properly. I have a "comics" and "books" folder, but they're not showing up in their explorer, and apparently the up and down arrows of the explorer do nothing. This is not much of an issue since you can just move your books/comics to the "download" folder for example.

TLDR: I don't know how this reader was in the early days, but right now it's the best way to read comics and books in VR. It's feature rich, has some nice environments and music, so highly recommended.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review - we appreciate it. We have just uploaded an updated version that enables access to all accessible folders on the Oculus GO. This should now enable you to access your "comics" and "books" folders.

reviews.review_description: So laggy it makes me feel sick.  Nothing wrong with my phone. No Bluetooth support for a controller (steelseries).  I knew it was a gamble spending 12$.  It's not worth 1$ at this point.

developer_response.body:
Kenny,
 immersionVR has been tested on all supported devices by our team and by Oculus and have not encountered a "lag" issue.
 Additionally we have not had any other reports regarding your problem.
 
 If you email us at immersionvr@optusnet.com.au with some specific details we will try and help you resolve your issue.
 
 In regard to the bluetooth controller we do not support this nor do we state that we do... immersionVR reader can be used with the gearVR controller for additional functionality however this is not required to use the application as all controls can be used in a hands-off mode.

reviews.review_description: please add options to change font type, more ebook formats, reader size and more themes

developer_response.body:
verioz,
 We are currently hard at work making additional upgrades to immersionVR reader. In the pipeline are support for additional book formats such as mobi, azw3 and pdf.
Additionally, support for comics formats, cbr and cbz will be implemented. 

In regard to reader size, the zoom function can be used to increase the size of the reader and additional themes can be added by the user thru the custom import feature.

Changing the font is something we hadn't considered ... but will now.
Thanks for your review.

[Update] The latest update supports alternate fonts

reviews.review_description: I had it in 2019 and i reinstalled it now. now it doesn't have the same themes and interface. that hands-free feature is horrible for gear vr if one has a controller, it should be switcheable.Where can I download themes? there were nice themes before.  I don't see any update since a long while ago. It could have much more comfortable options.
I love this app. It is exactly what I was looking for. The ambient sounds are a perfect addition to creating a peaceful reading environment.

I have tried to import books to this app after your feedback but the think is that from 3 books I succeed with only one. 2 of 3 books cause freezing and blocking of the entire gearVR system. What is wrong with this app? Smaller books can be imported but the big one (above 100mb) like human anatomy causing  freezing of the screen. My phone is Note 8 and I think that I have enough power to import something big. Also when I'm importing a book the app is very laggy and one can't do anything until the process is not finished!

developer_response.body:
Tuebor,
 The user guide is included in your library when you install immersionVR reader. 
You can also download a copy in PDF format from www.immersionVR-reader.com/user-guide. 
Unfortunately immersionVR reader itself does not yet support PDF books, it requires drm-free epub formatted books. 
More information about new features and FAQ can be found on www.immersionVR-reader.com
[Update] Tuebor - can you email me the details of the books that are causing issues thanks and I will try and help - email : immersionvr@optusnet.com.au

reviews.review_description: Buy if you enjoy being fooled.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the highly detailed review. Perhaps you had your headset on backwards.....

reviews.review_description:
Some things seem a little off 😢. The hands-free feature is nice for those who do not have any type of controller, but is a bit anoying for us who do. Staring at a blue dot in center of screen is a bit annoying. 
For those people who have a controller, can we have the option to disable the hands-free portion? I’d rather use controller only (I use an oculus go). Taking full advantage of the controller would be a big plus.
The swiping motion on the oculus go controller thumb pad to page forward or backwards seems to be backwards to me. I have to swipe left to right to advance the pages forward, that does not make sense because if you turn the pages of a book from left to right you turn the pages backwards. Controller actions should mimic real world book actions shouldn’t they?
Load time for .epub files seems very long. As I have over 100 books I will not be using this app a lot as it would take days to load it all.
I’d wait for revisions before buying. 😢

developer_response.body:
Hi, we are currently updating the application to provide the option to use either the controller or the gaze function. Additional functionality including performance improvements are also in the works.
The swiping motion is under discussion with the team but your argument does have merit.... maybe next release :)
